_meta.description: Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) mapping for Atlas topics
_meta.note: NGSS organizes standards by Disciplinary Core Ideas (DCI), Science & Engineering Practices (SEP), and Crosscutting Concepts (CCC). This maps tutor topics to Performance Expectations (PE).

Weather & Climate.description: Develop understanding of weather and climate, including factors that affect regional climates

Earth's Structure & Plate Tectonics.description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of Earth's materials and plate tectonics

Ecosystems & Energy Flow.description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence for the effects of resource availability on organisms

Properties of Matter.description: Develop models to describe the atomic composition of simple molecules and extended structures

Intro to Forces & Motion.description: Apply Newton's Third Law to design a solution to a problem involving the motion of two colliding objects

Space: The Solar System.description: Develop and use a model of the Earth-sun-moon system to describe cyclic patterns

Cell Structure & Function.description: Conduct investigation to show that living things are made of cells, and cells function to support life

Human Body Systems.description: Develop a model to describe how food is rearranged through chemical reactions to support growth

Genetics & Heredity.description: Develop and use a model to describe why structural changes to genes may affect proteins

Chemical Reactions (Intro).description: Analyze and interpret data on properties of substances before and after interaction

Waves & Electromagnetic Spectrum.description: Use mathematical representations to describe wave properties and interactions

Ecology & Biodiversity.description: Construct explanations for patterns of interactions among organisms across ecosystems

Forces & Motion.description: Plan investigation to provide evidence that change in motion depends on net force and object mass

Energy & Waves.description: Construct and interpret data and graphs to describe relationships of kinetic and potential energy

Matter & Chemical Reactions.description: Develop models to describe atomic composition; analyze substances before and after reactions

Cell Biology & Genetics.description: Develop and use models of cell structure, function, and genetic inheritance

Ecology & Ecosystems.description: Analyze data to provide evidence for effects of resource availability on ecosystems

Earth Systems & Space.description: Develop models of Earth-sun-moon system and Earth's internal processes

Atomic Structure & Periodic Table.description: Use the periodic table as a model to predict properties of elements based on electron patterns

Chemical Bonding & Reactions.description: Construct explanations about the outcome of chemical reactions including energy changes

Stoichiometry.description: Use mathematical representations to support the claim that atoms are conserved in reactions

States of Matter & Gas Laws.description: Plan investigation to determine relationships among pressure, volume, and temperature of gas

Acids, Bases & Solutions.description: Design and evaluate a solution to a complex real-world problem based on chemical processes

Nuclear Chemistry (Intro).description: Develop models to illustrate changes in nuclear composition and the energy released

Cell Biology & Molecular Processes.description: Construct explanations for the role of specific cell structures in organism function

Genetics & DNA.description: Ask questions to clarify relationships about the role of DNA and chromosomes in inheritance

Evolution & Natural Selection.description: Communicate scientific information that common ancestry is supported by multiple lines of evidence

Ecology & Environmental Science.description: Evaluate claims and evidence about effects of group behavior on individual and species survival

Human Anatomy & Physiology.description: Develop and use a model based on evidence to illustrate hierarchical organization of systems

Biotechnology (Intro).description: Evaluate technological solutions and their impact on biological systems

Kinematics & Dynamics.description: Analyze data to support the claim that Newton's second law describes the mathematical relationship

Work, Energy & Power.description: Create a computational model to calculate change between kinetic and potential energy

Waves & Optics.description: Use mathematical representations to support a claim regarding relationships among wave properties

Electricity & Magnetism.description: Plan and conduct investigation to provide evidence that electric current produces magnetic force

Thermodynamics.description: Plan and conduct investigation to provide evidence that energy transfer is related to temperature

Modern Physics (Intro).description: Evaluate the claims and evidence about electromagnetic radiation and matter interactions

Advanced Mechanics.description: Advanced application of Newton's laws and mathematical modeling of motion systems

Electromagnetism.description: Evaluate evidence for electromagnetic force interactions and wave-particle behavior

Quantum Physics (Intro).description: Evaluate claims about electromagnetic radiation absorption and emission by atoms (beyond NGSS — AP level)

Organic Chemistry (Intro).description: Apply understanding of chemical structures to carbon-based molecules

Biochemistry.description: Construct explanations based on evidence for how carbon cycles through organisms and ecosystems

Environmental Science & Sustainability.description: Evaluate or refine a technological solution that reduces impacts of human activity on natural systems
